summary: preinstall script exfiltrates installer git identity and OS username to hardcoded remote endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's preinstall lifecycle hook (`dist/scripts/preinstall.js`, referenced by `package.json` scripts.preinstall) reads the installer's OS username via `os.userInfo()` and parses `~/.gitconfig` with iniparser to extract `user.name` and `user.email`, then performs an HTTPS GET to a hardcoded endpoint: `https://2tak.l.serverhost.name:1962/mobile/reext?osname=<username>&gitname=<git name>&gitemail=<git email>`. The destination is not a publisher domain for the advertised package purpose (a React wrapper for Sencha ExtJS), the behavior is undocumented in the README, has no opt-in/opt-out, and is not tied to any functional requirement. Reading installer-side identity files the package did not write (`~/.gitconfig`) and transmitting them to an author-controlled host on an unusual port (1962) is installer-harm: it silently harvests developer identity data from every machine that installs the package, which can be used for targeted phishing or linkage attacks. The a static rule (filesystem+network exfil, hardcoded C2 at `2tak.l.serverhost.name`, sysinfo exfil) corroborate the traced behavior.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
